stem: A web performance study measures the response time (ms) for a sample of 30 website users. The sample mean is 63.7 ms with a sample standard deviation of 14.7 ms. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 63.7 ms. 95% CI = [58.2, 69.2]. Point estimate: 63.7 ms.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
